essence: David suspects Claude applied repository changes to an outdated copy of AppyRadar central because its home had already been moved to a different location.

actions.text: Verify which copy of AppyRadar central received the changes and reconcile with the relocated repo

essence: David is unsure how to complete a 'W Merge' brain rebuild — whether he must go into KBDE and run a rebuild or act in the current session — and gets no confirmed answer.

Reviewing spec work not seen since yesterday, David confirms the work is good but his examples fit poorly, notes Ian's people/messaging-relationship focus would get more value, asks whether the recent schema-update migration system applies, and approves proceeding on the AI's recommendation.

actions.text: Proceed with the recommended spec work and check if a schema update/migration is required

essence: Chiang Mai AI meetup digests Fable 5's US-government suspension hours after release and the insight (via Ian's Tokyo week with Boris Cherny et al.) that Anthropic cut ~80% of the system prompt — declaring prompt/context engineering dead in favour of loop engineering: short open-ended North-Star goals, nested sub-agents, file-based memory, plus the hard 1M-token context ceiling and Anthropic's strategy-free bottoms-up culture; David describes his AngelEye/AppyRadar MCP constellation feeding live system intelligence into loops.

actions.text: Review the Anthropic connector portal before recommending it to clients

essence: Over pre-meetup lunch, David and peers unpack the sudden US-government shutdown of Fable model access (VPN blocks, possible KYC/passport-based citizenship gating) and David mentions using it to build a visual explorer for his brain — node/graph views of ingestion and retrieval layers — on a $20 plan, with a long run possibly interrupted by the cutoff.

David and his NDIS partner scope and greenlight the 'Daily Support Plan' (DSP) app — ingest behaviour support plans, allied health reports and incident CSVs, shard/extract per-item plus holistic observations with provenance, human accept/correct/flag review with a context-aware chat pane, output the loved one-page-plus PDF guide — built as a one-shot on AppyStack under Supporting Potential as a $400-1000-per-plan cashflow tool and bridge into SupportSignal subscriptions; Fable 5 was suspended mid-setup so the /goal run kicked off on Opus 4.8 at extreme effort.

actions.text: One-shot build the Daily Support Plan app under the Supporting Potential folder using AppyStack
Commit and push the DSP app to a private repo under David's organisation and grant the partner access
Partner to apply for the Anthropic Leaders program as an NDIS domain lead

essence: Studying a YouTube video that Claude Fable 5 one-shotted end-to-end (script from announcement research, ElevenLabs voice clone in sub-minute chunks, avatar-5 renders, FFmpeg stitching, HTML/GSAP motion graphics in hyperframes with visual self-review), David queues research on file-based memory vs his KDD approach, the motion-graphics tool vs Remotion, locating his AppyDave/AI-TLDR transcript catalogues, and complains app requirements shouldn't live under brand-dave — wanting a dedicated brain area for new-app planning feeding the dark-factory pipeline.

actions.text: Background-research the hyperframes-style motion graphics tool vs Remotion and find existing skills for it
Locate the AppyDave and AI-TLDR video transcript catalogues across both machines
Research Boris Cherny's plan-less, file-memory way of building apps as input to the DSP one-shot

essence: David sets the dark-factory build campaign: an app per day with Fable 5 until June 22 under an 'AI-native company / software automation factory' content theme — every app built recipe-first (Symphony-style one-shot recipes with a self-improving recipe builder) on AppyStack/AppySentinel foundations, recipes given away as Skool community monetisation; slated apps include the AppyDave thumbnail builder (title/design/thumbnail-text/subliminal-text model via media studio and kie.ai), AngelEye Sentinel (session watcher, 28-of-30 capturable hooks, MCP+HTTP), AppyRadar, Switchboard (routing/control plane), Watchtower (factory visualisation), a school/community asset builder, and YLO the YouTube launch optimiser.

actions.text: Build the AppyDave thumbnail builder app (title + design + thumbnail text + subliminal text) using prior thumbnail research
Build AngelEye Sentinel with hook capture, MCP server and API
Build the YouTube launch optimiser (YLO) micro app

essence: David vents that the dark factory is too fragile and never self-managing as promised — Claude makes patchwork fixes without architectural thinking, roadmap/north-star visibility is missing, and parallel sessions conflict — and weighs options: manually building AppyRadar/Switchboard/Watchtower first, re-reviewing every document and commit for drift with a Q&A back-on-track pass, and using scarce Fable 5 credits sparingly to unify docs/memories then delegating builds to the M4 Mini.
